assertions.says: Running proof with no arguments shows a table of proof history
The table includes a Result column
The table includes an Assertions column
Each row shows the slug of a completed work item
Each row shows the date the work was completed
Entries are sorted with most recent first
Assertion ratio shows satisfied count out of total
When no proofs exist yet, the output says so without erroring
Missing proof chain file exits successfully
An empty proof chain also shows the no-proofs message
An empty proof chain exits successfully
JSON list mode outputs valid JSON
JSON list mode includes the entries array
JSON list with missing file returns empty entries
Detail view still works when a slug is provided
Detail view still shows the feature name
Detail JSON still works with a slug
A single entry renders a table without crashing
Entries missing a completion timestamp sort to the end
The scan result includes a project kind classification
Scanning a Node CLI project with a bin field detects it as a CLI tool
Detection works without reading the filesystem
New projects default to unknown kind until classified
A project with a bin field in package.json is classified as a CLI tool
A project with CLI dependencies like commander is classified as a CLI tool
A project with a browser framework like React is classified as a web app
A project with a server framework and no browser UI is classified as an API server
A project with both server and browser frameworks is classified as full-stack
A project exporting a library without CLI or framework signals is classified as a library
A project with no recognizable signals is classified as unknown
Non-Node projects are not falsely classified
The bin field takes priority over library export markers
CLI dependencies take priority over library export markers
Project descriptions use the kind for accurate labels like CLI tool or library
The census tracks whether each package declares CLI binaries

callouts.summary: `truncate` word-boundary behavior for CJK: — readme.ts:65-69. When text has no spaces before `cap`, falls back to hard-cut at `cap` characters. Correct for Latin text; may split mid-character for CJK

A009 contract deviation: — Contract specifies `value: 5000` for total cap. Implementation now uses 4000 to make the cap reachable. The original 5000 was unreachable dead code (3 × 1500 = 4500 < 5000).

A014 monorepo test verifies intent not mechanism: — readme.test.ts:157-171. `detectReadme(tmpDir)` has no monorepo concept — it reads from the path it's given. The test proves scan-engine's decision t

A004 tests serialization, not e2e scan: — readme.test.ts:69-84. The test verifies the ReadmeResult serializes correctly to JSON, not that `ana scan --json` produces the field in CLI output. An e2e tes
Contract A009 value should be updated: — The contract still says `value: 5000` but the implementation is 4000. If the contract is re-sealed in the future, update the value.
